     Repos audited: SaaSWrld/RocketPlate @ f33ab3d ("RP") and Dreone6/rocketplate-daily-delights @ HEAD ("DD"). -->

# 02 — Page / Feature / Component / API / Flow Matrix

**52 items.** Dispositions: **keep** 39 · **merge** 1 · **refactor** 4 · **archive** 5 · **remove** 3. `LC` = launch-critical.
Disposition applies to the consolidated product: which implementation survives and what happens to the other.

## Pages

| Item | In DD | In RP | Disposition | LC | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Brand landing page (rocketplate.io persona)** | /company route (src/routes/company.tsx, 603 lines) — fully built, richer JSON-LD/OG head, hero video slot stubbed, on Lovable hosting + second backend | index.html (1,928 lines) — deployed on Netlify site 'rocketplate', full SEO + JSON-LD, 3D rocket, smoke-tested on real deploy | **KEEP** | ✅ | RP version is live, verified (smoke.mjs route + backend checks), wired to the hardened uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof backend, and carries the approved Warm Launch art direction. DD /company is a duplicate brand page on a parallel stack; archive it with the DD repo. Rebuilding the live page on DD's stack mid-Phase-Zero violates ADR-001 (static-first until Gate C) for zero demand-validation gain. |
| **Conversion landing page (gorocketplate.com persona)** | / route (src/routes/index.tsx, 843 lines) — polished waitlist site, all CTAs open WaitlistDialog, writes to wrong backend | gorocketplate/index.html (1,249 lines) — live on Netlify site 'gorocketplate', full analytics dictionary, quick-capture, ZIP checker, referral suite | **KEEP** | ✅ | RP go site is the variant-test arm of ADR-004 feeding the single production leads table via upsert_lead. DD's version submits leads into Supabase project bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc — a second database that splits the Miami 33186 demand signal Phase Zero exists to measure. Archive DD's page. |
| **Restaurant partners page + application form** | absent — restaurant_partner_applications table exists in DD's DB but no UI writes to it; restaurants funnel into leads as role restaurant_owner | partners/index.html (545 lines): marketing sections, 15-field form via submit_partner_application RPC, tokenized status view | **KEEP** | ✅ | Only implementation with an actual supply-side intake UI, and it is RPC-only per ADR-002. Supply-side validation (restaurants willing to batch 2-4 PM) is half the Phase-Zero thesis. DD's orphan table dies with its backend. |
| **/partner/ legacy redirect stub** | absent | partner/index.html (16 lines, noindex) + netlify.toml 301 preserving ?token; smoke.mjs asserts the 301 | **KEEP** | — | Cheap insurance for old links and emailed status tokens; already covered by CI smoke test. |
| **Admin metrics dashboard** | absent as UI — DB has admin RLS policies (has_role) but no admin page or server code | admin/index.html (194 lines): bcrypt-token gate, admin_dashboard RPC (totals/zones/roles/cuisines/variants/recent leads), CSV exports | **KEEP** | ✅ | This is how the founder reads the demand signal daily — the entire point of Phase Zero. DD ships admin database machinery with no cockpit. RP's is read-only, escaped, token-gated. |
| **Account self-service page (lead preferences)** | absent | account/index.html (176 lines): ?token= → get_my_lead / update_my_lead, referral link + count, noindex + robots-disallowed | **KEEP** | ✅ | upsert_lead responses and confirmation emails link to it; a 404 here breaks the post-signup experience. One refactor note: SITE constant (line ~90) still points at rocketplate.netlify.app pending DNS cutover — already tracked in LAUNCH-REVIEW. |
| **Contact routing page** | absent (footer mailto only) | contact/index.html (34 lines): 4 routing cards, indexable, in sitemap; broken ../assets/logo.png favicon (line 8) | **KEEP** | — | Low-stakes utility page; fix the favicon ref in the same pass as the partners logo fix. |
| **Privacy + Terms pages** | absent | privacy/index.html + terms/index.html (39 lines each) — plain-language placeholders; noindex meta yet listed in sitemap.xml; broken ../assets/logo.png favicon (line 9 each) | **REFACTOR** | ✅ | Keep RP pages but three fixes are owed: (1) formal legal + TCPA sign-off before paid traffic / SMS sends (open launch-checklist item 7); (2) resolve the noindex-vs-sitemap contradiction; (3) fix the favicon path. Consent capture in both lead forms depends on these pages existing and being defensible. |
| **404 page + SSR error boundary** | __root.tsx 404 component + server.ts/error-capture.ts/error-page.ts pipeline reporting into Lovable (__lovableEvents) | none custom — Netlify default 404 | **REMOVE** | — | DD's error plumbing is Lovable-platform-coupled and meaningless off Lovable hosting. Static Netlify sites need at most a custom 404.html (a 10-minute nice-to-have, not worth porting React error boundaries for). |

## Features

| Item | In DD | In RP | Disposition | LC | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Lead capture form (primary conversion surface)** | LeadForm.tsx (351 lines): Zod client validation, role-conditional fields, live ZIP classifier — but submits via direct anon INSERT on leads (leads.ts:155) with a silently-failing anon UPDATE fallback on duplicates (no anon UPDATE policy), no rate limiting | go-site #leadForm (full capture, progress bar, per-field errors) + flagship #join short form — both → upsert_lead SECURITY DEFINER RPC: server-side validation, rate-limited, ON CONFLICT dup-graceful, first-touch attribution preserved | **KEEP** | ✅ | RP's write path is the constitution (ADR-002, hard rule 1) and was verified 2026-07-19 (raw inserts 42501, RPC 200). DD's path violates it outright and lies to duplicate users ('updated your preferences' while 0 rows change). Field-for-field, RP's go-site form already matches or exceeds DD's richness. Archive DD's form. |
| **ZIP checker** | classifyZip() in leads.ts — client-only classification preview inside the form, mirrors launch_zones seed, no live count | both sites: zone verdict + real neighbor count via zip_wait_count RPC (only intentional public aggregate), prefills form ZIP, tracked events | **KEEP** | ✅ | RP adds real social proof from the production database — an honest counter that complies with the no-fake-data rule. DD's preview-only version adds nothing RP lacks. |
| **Referral loop** | DB trigger generates real referral_code but RLS means it is never returned; UI shows a cosmetic /?ref=<firstName> link — referrals are not actually attributable | upsert_lead returns the server-generated referral_code; go-site success state renders real ?ref= link, copy button, X/WhatsApp/Facebook/mailto shares; referral_count on leads; ?ref captured as referred_by | **KEEP** | ✅ | RP's loop is real and measurable end-to-end; DD's is decorative and would corrupt referral attribution — borderline fake-data under hard rule 5. Remove DD's implementation with the repo. |
| **Restaurant referral capture (success state)** | restaurant_referral free-text field on the lead form (stored on the lead row) | #restoRefForm → refer_restaurant validated RPC into restaurant_referrals table (hardening batch, commit 5fb6d18) | **KEEP** | — | RP's is a first-class, rate-limited, queryable table; DD's is a text column on leads. Keep RP. |
| **Quick capture hero form (go site)** | absent (dialog-based capture instead) | #quickForm: ZIP+email prefill → scroll to full form, no network call, tracked | **KEEP** | — | Zero-backend-risk friction reducer unique to RP. |
| **Partner application status tracker** | absent | partners/?token= → get_my_application, 5-stage pipeline render (new→reviewing→tasting→menu_setup→live) + declined/on_hold banners | **KEEP** | — | Unique RP differentiator; token comes back from submit_partner_application so breaking it would strand applicants. |
| **UTM / attribution capture** | lib/utm.ts — sessionStorage persistence (rp_attribution_v1), first-touch UTMs / latest-ref, SSR-safe; well-engineered | query-param capture at page load on both sites; upsert_lead preserves first-touch attribution server-side on conflict | **KEEP** | — | RP's server-side first-touch guarantee is the authoritative half and survives; DD's session persistence adds little on single-page landing sites. If cross-page attribution ever matters (more RP pages joining the funnel), port the sessionStorage pattern — note it in the archive README. |
| **Analytics events + GTM integration** | none (only Lovable error reporting) | rpTrack + full data-track dictionary on both sites matching DNS-AND-ANALYTICS.md; guarded GTM stubs with placeholder ID (GTM-XXXXXXX / GTM_ID) in 3 files | **KEEP** | ✅ | Funnel measurement is what a validation phase produces. Event system is complete; the GTM container ID is founder-gated launch-checklist item 4 — events currently push to a dead dataLayer. |
| **Motion / scroll system** | Lenis via useSmoothScroll + useReveal IntersectionObserver hooks, per its own INTERACTION_DIRECTION.md; reduced-motion disciplined | GSAP + ScrollTrigger + Lenis 3-tier system per INTERACTION-DIRECTION.md (word-split heads, card group beats, .rv reveals), reduced-motion kill switch, matches shipped spec | **KEEP** | — | Both are good; RP's is the one attached to the surviving pages. DD's React hooks are stack-coupled — archive as Gate-C references. |
| **Three.js scroll-choreographed rocket (flagship)** | absent — DD's own doc explicitly declined Three.js ('cost/benefit doesn't clear the bar') | procedural rocket + orbiting plate, keyframe scroll choreography, requestIdleCallback + desktop-only injection, reduced-motion pinned | **KEEP** | — | Brand signature with disciplined loading policy; zero external 3D assets; already perf-gated per hard rule 7. |
| **Hero video system** | HeroVideo.tsx + CinematicHero.tsx (well-engineered React) but reference /assets/rocketplate-hero.* which do not exist in DD's public/ — permanently on fallback | HEAD-gated desktop-only activation on both sites; real webm/mp4/poster assets deployed and immutable-cached; smoke test fetches the webm | **KEEP** | — | RP's version is live with real media; DD's is dead weight until assets that live in RP's repo anyway. Nothing to merge. |
| **DD application stack (TanStack Start SSR + React 19 + TS strict + Bun)** | the entire repo: modern, strict, SSR-capable, well-crafted frontend engineering | deliberately absent — vanilla static per ADR-001, framework decision deferred to Gate C | **ARCHIVE** | — | This is DD's real asset and the disposition headline: park the repo read-only (it is currently only in a temp scratchpad path — move it somewhere durable) as the leading Gate-C framework candidate, referenced from ADR-005. Do NOT maintain it in parallel; parallel implementations are how the two-backend problem happened. |

## Apis

| Item | In DD | In RP | Disposition | LC | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Production Supabase backend (uhmyjevtfrynunxfsyof)** | absent — DD never touches it | 7 tables, 11+ SECURITY DEFINER RPCs, rate-limit trigger, email queue + edge function, admin views; hardened 2026-07-19, advisors clean, verified via smoke test | **KEEP** | ✅ | The single system of record for the consolidated product. Every surviving surface already points at it. |
| **Public write RPCs (upsert_lead, submit_partner_application, refer_restaurant)** | none — browser-direct table INSERT (leads.ts:155), violating ADR-002; no server functions exist despite scaffolding | all three deployed, server-validated, rate-limited, dup-graceful; verified 2026-07-19 | **KEEP** | ✅ | Hard rule 1. RP's RPC layer is the non-negotiable core of the consolidated product. |
| **Rate limiting (submission_log + rate_limit_guard trigger)** | none anywhere — schema CHECKs only | 10 submissions / 10 min / IP on leads, partner apps, referrals; friendly rate_limited copy in all forms | **KEEP** | ✅ | Only defense against waitlist-count pollution by scripts; without it the demand signal is untrustworthy. |
| **Token-gated per-record RPCs (get_my_lead, update_my_lead, get_my_application)** | none | deployed; power /account and the partner status tracker; capability tokens as RPC args, no cookies/auth | **KEEP** | ✅ | Pragmatic pre-auth pattern explicitly blessed by ADR-002 until Gate C introduces Supabase Auth + RBAC. |
| **Admin auth model** | user_roles table + has_role() SECURITY DEFINER + admin RLS policies — a Supabase-Auth RBAC skeleton with no users, no UI, no server code | admin_credentials single-row bcrypt token + is_admin/admin_* RPCs; sessionStorage session; rotation SQL documented in BACKEND.md | **KEEP** | ✅ | RP's capability-token model is live and sufficient for one operator in Phase Zero. DD's user_roles/has_role design is the better long-term shape — archive it as the reference implementation for the Gate-C RBAC migration ADR-002 already schedules. Note: the admin token pasted in chat 2026-07-07 still needs rotation (launch checklist). |
| **leads table schema** | ~30-col parallel schema, 18 CHECK constraints, unique lower(email), referral_code trigger, updated_at trigger | equivalent columns plus access_token, feeding_count, subscribe_timeline; unique lower(email); validation lives in upsert_lead instead of CHECKs; updated_at trigger | **KEEP** | ✅ | RP's schema is the wired one. Before decommissioning DD, run a column diff to confirm nothing worth porting (DD's per-column CHECK style is a reasonable belt-and-suspenders idea RP handles in the RPC — optional hardening, not a blocker). |
| **launch_zones table + client ZONES config** | 10-ZIP Miami seed (33186 priority + 9 nearby), public SELECT, status enum incl. paused | 13-ZIP seed (33186 priority + 12 nearby), anon SELECT, no count columns (private totals stay admin-side); duplicated client-side ZONES arrays in both sites' JS | **KEEP** | — | Keep RP's table and seed. Known refactor debt (acknowledged in BUILD-NOTES): client ZONES lists duplicate the DB seed and must be hand-synced — post-launch, have pages read launch_zones or generate the constant. Not launch-blocking. |
| **Email pipeline (email_events + queue triggers + send-emails edge function)** | email_events table exists but nothing writes it; Resend integration is a code comment ('fire from a createServerFn') — not implemented | queue_lead_emails trigger on insert, 7 event types mapped, deployed ACTIVE edge function with selftest (smoke-tested), dormant pending RESEND_API_KEY + domain + cron (founder-gated) | **KEEP** | ✅ | RP is one founder action from live confirmation emails; DD would need the whole pipeline built. Waitlist confirmation + Founding-100 nurture are conversion-critical for validation quality. |
| **Admin aggregate views** | launch_zone_stats (security_invoker) — broken for anon by construction, consumed by nothing | admin_leads_by_zip, admin_cuisine_demand, admin_zone_stats — definer-side, consumed by admin_dashboard, not granted to anon | **KEEP** | — | RP's views are working and correctly private; DD's view is a defect (invoker rights vs admin-only SELECT) and dies with the project. |
| **Smoke tests + CI (scripts/smoke.mjs, smoke.yml, supabase-keepalive.yml)** | none — no tests of any kind | zero-dependency smoke covering 10+ routes, 301, security headers, zip_wait_count RPC, edge-fn selftest; GitHub workflows tracked (activate on push — blocked by open issue 5, stale GitHub credential) | **KEEP** | ✅ | Embodies hard rule 4 (verify on the real deploy). The unpushed-remote single-laptop risk is the top non-founder engineering risk in the whole portfolio. |
| **DD SSR/server scaffolding (server.ts, start.ts, client.server.ts, auth-middleware.ts)** | Nitro server entry + error recovery, service-role client, JWT middleware — all scaffolded, zero createServerFn usage in the codebase | n/a — static sites, no server tier | **ARCHIVE** | — | Unused infrastructure whose only value is if Gate C adopts an SSR framework; it is Lovable/TanStack-specific so treat as reference, not a head start. |
| **DD Supabase project (bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc) — second backend** | full parallel schema: leads (~30 cols, referral trigger), launch_zones (10-ZIP seed), restaurant_partner_applications, email_events, user_roles, waitlist_signups, launch_zone_stats view; anon INSERT WITH CHECK policies; confirmed in DD/.env + supabase/config.toml | absent | **REMOVE** | ✅ | Two backends cannot both survive — every lead captured here is invisible to RP's admin dashboard and splits the 33186 demand signal. Decommission ORDER MATTERS: (1) export any real rows from DD leads/waitlist_signups, (2) replay them through upsert_lead into production (preserves validation + referral codes + email queue), (3) take the Lovable site offline or repoint it, (4) pause/delete the project. Do not delete before the export check. |
| **Legacy waitlist tables + dead client code** | waitlist_signups table + lib/waitlist.ts (imported nowhere, grep-confirmed) | legacy `waitlist` table from first migration, not documented as active | **REMOVE** | — | Dead code on both sides. DD's disappears with its project (after the export check, since it may hold week-one signups). RP's should be dropped via a named migration per hard rule 3, or explicitly documented as retired in BACKEND.md. |

## Flows

| Item | In DD | In RP | Disposition | LC | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Signup flow (end-to-end)** | form → direct anon INSERT → no email, silently-broken duplicate path, cosmetic referral link, no rate limit, wrong database | form → upsert_lead (validate, rate-limit, upsert, first-touch) → email_events queued → success state with real referral link + account token link → account self-service | **KEEP** | ✅ | RP's flow is complete, verified, and observable in the admin dashboard. This is THE flow Phase Zero exists to run; DD's version fails on three of five stages. |
| **Partner apply flow (end-to-end)** | absent (orphan table only) | partners form → submit_partner_application → access_token status link → get_my_application pipeline view → admin_dashboard partners table + CSV export | **KEEP** | ✅ | Only existing implementation; validates the supply side of the marketplace. |
| **Admin flow (end-to-end)** | absent | token gate → admin_dashboard aggregates + tables → CSV exports; unauthorized clears session; Lock button | **KEEP** | ✅ | Operational cockpit for the validation experiment; read-only by design. |

## CTO consolidation verdict (from the matrix agent)

CONSOLIDATION VERDICT (CTO summary): RP (RocketPlate.Build) is the consolidated product in its entirety — every page, flow, RPC, asset, and doc that ships for the Miami 33186 Phase-Zero launch is RP's. DD is archived whole: its two pages, form, components, and stack are a well-built second implementation of a product RP already ships, wired to a second Supabase backend (bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc) through a write path (direct anon INSERT at src/lib/leads.ts:155, verified) that violates ADR-002 and RP hard rule 1. Nothing from DD is launch-critical; nothing from DD blocks launch by being archived.

DECOMMISSION SEQUENCE (the one destructive step — order matters): (1) query DD's leads + waitlist_signups for real signups collected during its 07-01→07-07 build week; (2) if any exist, replay them through production upsert_lead (preserves validation, referral codes, email queueing, first-touch attribution); (3) take the Lovable deployment offline or repoint it so no live page keeps writing to the dead backend; (4) pause/delete Supabase project bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc. I could not verify from the repo whether DD holds real leads — treat the export check as mandatory, not optional.

WHAT DD ACTUALLY CONTRIBUTES to the consolidated product: (a) the Gate-C framework candidate (archive repo read-only in a durable location — it currently lives only under a session temp scratchpad; record as ADR-005); (b) user_roles + has_role RBAC skeleton as the reference design for the Supabase Auth migration ADR-002 already schedules pre-Gate-C; (c) motion-spec ideas worth merging into RP's INTERACTION-DIRECTION.md; (d) optionally 3 food JPGs and the utm.ts sessionStorage pattern. That is the complete merge surface.

LAUNCH-CRITICAL FIXES SURFACED BY THIS AUDIT (RP-side, engineering-doable now): (1) broken ../assets/logo.png favicon/logo refs on partners/privacy/terms/contact — grep-verified at partners/index.html:21,200,432, privacy:9, terms:9, contact:8 — visible 404 on the partners page header/footer; flagship index.html also has no favicon link at all; (2) noindex-vs-sitemap contradiction on privacy/terms; (3) push the repo to a remote (LAUNCH-REVIEW open issue 5) — single-laptop risk also gates the smoke/keepalive workflows. Everything else launch-critical is founder-gated per the LAUNCH-REVIEW checklist (DNS, GTM ID, Resend activation, admin token rotation, formal legal/TCPA, one real end-to-end signup per site).

DOC DEBT TO CLEAR IN THE CONSOLIDATION COMMIT (hard rule 6): write ADR-005 recording this decision; reconcile BACKEND.md's migration list; rewrite gorocketplate/BUILD-NOTES.md's pre-hardening DB section; fix the stale architecture comment at gorocketplate/index.html:826.

Audit was strictly read-only; all matrix rows are grounded in the three inventories, with the four highest-consequence claims re-verified directly against both repos (second Supabase project ref in DD/.env + supabase/config.toml; DD direct table write; RP RPC-only call sites across all 6 backend-calling files; RP broken logo refs).

## Ground-check addendum (independent verify agent)

READ-ONLY AUDIT COMPLETE. 12 rows sampled. Note up front: the provided matrix JSON contains only 4 of the 6 dispositions (keep/refactor/archive/remove — zero rows are marked "merge" or "rebuild"), and the JSON is truncated mid-row (the "Partner apply flow" row cuts off at `"disposition":"keep",`), so any rows after it were unverifiable.

## Per-row verification

**1. Brand landing page (keep) — ACCURATE (minor off-by-one).** DD `src/routes/company.tsx` = 602 lines (claimed 603); has OG tags, `application/ld+json`, and the hero-video slot stubbed exactly as claimed (CinematicHero `videoSrc` usage commented out at lines 113-115). RP `index.html` = 1,928 lines exactly.

**2. Conversion landing page (keep) — ACCURATE (minor off-by-one).** DD `src/routes/index.tsx` = 842 lines (claimed 843), 9 WaitlistDialog references (all CTAs dialog-based). RP `gorocketplate/index.html` = 1,248 lines (claimed 1,249). DD "wrong backend" confirmed: `.env` sets `VITE_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID="bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc"`.

**3. Privacy + Terms (refactor) — ACCURATE.** Both exactly 39 lines; `<meta name="robots" content="noindex">` at line 8 yet both listed in `sitemap.xml` (lines 6-7); `<link rel="icon" href="../assets/logo.png">` at line 9 in each, and `C:\Users\dreon\RocketPlate.Build\assets\logo.png` does not exist (logo.png lives at repo root) — favicon broken as claimed. All three owed fixes are real.

**4. 404 + SSR error boundary (remove) — ACCURATE.** DD `__root.tsx` has `NotFoundComponent` (line 15) wired as `notFoundComponent` (line 117); `src/server.ts`, `src/lib/error-capture.ts`, `src/lib/error-page.ts`, `src/lib/lovable-error-reporting.ts` all exist; `__lovableEvents` coupling confirmed in `__root.tsx` + `lovable-error-reporting.ts`. RP has no custom 404 file.

**5. Lead capture form (keep) — ACCURATE, including the exact line number.** DD `src/lib/leads.ts:155` is literally `const { error } = await supabase.from("leads").insert(payload);`. The duplicate path does an anon `.update(...)` (lines 160-183) while migrations grant `FOR UPDATE TO authenticated` only — no anon UPDATE policy anywhere — so it silently updates 0 rows yet returns "We updated your preferences." Claim verified. RP: `upsert_lead` present in both `index.html` and `gorocketplate/index.html` (1 call each). LeadForm.tsx = 350 lines (claimed 351).

**6. Referral loop (keep) — ACCURATE.** DD migration `20260707222144...sql` creates `leads_set_referral_code` trigger + `generate_referral_code()`, but leads has no anon SELECT policy so the code is never returned; DD UI builds the cosmetic link at `LeadForm.tsx:272`: `` `${origin}/?ref=${encodeURIComponent(firstName.toLowerCase())}` `` — firstName-based, exactly as claimed. RP account page consumes real `referral_code` from `get_my_lead` (account/index.html:122).

**7. shadcn/Radix suite (archive) — ACCURATE.** Exactly 46 files in DD `src/components/ui/`; `tailwindcss: ^4.2.1` in package.json. RP has no component framework, only per-page CSS + `dash.css` (73 lines, matching its own row).

**8. DD Supabase project bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc (remove) — ACCURATE.** `.env` and `supabase/config.toml` both pin `bnqxickbbsunlmuierkc`. 5 migrations create: `waitlist_signups` (+ "Anyone can join waitlist" anon INSERT), `leads` (+ anon INSERT `WITH CHECK`), `user_roles`/`has_role()` SECURITY DEFINER, `launch_zones` (10-ZIP seed, 33186 priority=100, enum includes 'paused'), `restaurant_partner_applications` (anon INSERT `WITH CHECK (true)`), `email_events`, `launch_zone_stats` view. No rate limiting anywhere in the migrations. One nuance: "~30 cols" on leads = 27 in CREATE TABLE + 4 ADD COLUMN ≈ 31 (fine); "18 CHECK constraints" is actually ~19 in the CREATE TABLE block — trivially off.

**9. Legacy waitlist tables + dead code (remove) — ACCURATE.** DD `lib/waitlist.ts` (37 lines) imported nowhere (grep confirmed zero imports). RP `BACKEND.md:134` lists `create_waitlist_table` as the first migration, and the BACKEND.md Tables section (leads, launch_zones, restaurant_partner_applications, email_events, submission_log) does not document a `waitlist` table as active — matches the claim.

**10. DD SSR/server scaffolding (archive) — ACCURATE.** `server.ts`, `start.ts`, `integrations/supabase/client.server.ts` (service-role), `auth-middleware.ts` all present; `createServerFn` appears exactly once in the whole codebase — as a comment (`leads.ts:195`). Zero actual usage confirmed.

**11. Hero video system (keep) — ACCURATE.** DD `HeroVideo.tsx` references `/assets/rocketplate-hero.webm|.mp4|poster` but DD `public/` contains only `favicon.ico` → permanently on fallback, as claimed. RP: HEAD-gating confirmed on both sites (flagship `index.html:1263` `fetch(url, {method:"HEAD"})` behind a 960px/reduced-motion gate at 1207-1208; go site line 882); real webm/mp4/poster files exist in both `assets/` dirs; `smoke.mjs` fetches both webms (lines 66, 72).

**12. Admin metrics dashboard (keep) — ACCURATE.** `admin/index.html` exactly 194 lines; sessionStorage token gate (line 80), `admin_dashboard` RPC (line 150), CSV exports via `admin_export_leads`/`admin_export_partners` (lines 188-189); bcrypt hashing lives in `admin_credentials` per BACKEND.md:75-84. DD has admin RLS via `has_role` with no UI — confirmed (no admin route exists in DD).

**Incidental spot-checks (all consistent with matrix):** zip_wait_count on both RP sites + `classifyZip` in DD; RP client `ZONES` array = 13 ZIPs (33186 + 12 nearby) duplicated in both sites' JS exactly as the launch_zones row admits; GTM placeholder (`GTM-XXXXXXX`/`GTM_ID`) in exactly 3 HTML files (index, gorocketplate/index, partners); DD `utm.ts` uses `rp_attribution_v1` in sessionStorage; DD hooks = Lenis + IntersectionObserver; RP flagship = gsap (19 refs) + ScrollTrigger (5) + lenis + THREE.* with `requestIdleCallback` injection (line 1753); DD's own `INTERACTION_DIRECTION.md:76` declines Three.js verbatim; DD `email_events` written by nothing outside generated types; RP email pipeline (`queue_lead_emails`, `send-emails` edge fn, RESEND_API_KEY-gated) documented in BACKEND.md:34,110-129. One nuance on the DD "Admin aggregate views" row: `launch_zone_stats` is `security_invoker = on` AND granted to anon — for anon it returns rows with zero-filtered counts rather than erroring, so "broken for anon by construction" is directionally right but slightly overstated; "consumed by nothing" confirmed.

## Missing rows (things the matrix never mentions)

1. **RP `.github/workflows/supabase-keepalive.yml`** — cron (Mon/Thu) pinging `zip_wait_count` because the free-tier project "silently died 2026-07-15 → restored 2026-07-18". Operationally critical infrastructure tied to the surviving backend; no matrix row.
2. **RP `.github/workflows/smoke.yml`** — the CI harness that runs smoke.mjs; the script is cited repeatedly but the workflow itself has no disposition.
3. **DD marketing photography + unresolved Lovable asset stubs** — `src/assets/chef-kitchen.jpg`, `doorstep-delivery.jpg`, `hot-meals-hero.jpg` (real images), plus `hero-cinematic-poster.jpg.asset.json` and `rocketplate-logo.svg.asset.json` pointing at Lovable-internal `/__l5e/` URLs. Real brand assets that vanish when DD is archived; no row.
4. **RP root `logo.png` vs the broken `../assets/logo.png` refs** — the matrix flags the broken favicon three times but never states the actual fix (the file exists at repo root, not in `assets/`); no row for the logo asset itself. Similarly `assets/food/*.jpg` (4 cuisine images) and `og.jpg` have no row.
5. **Docs corpora on both sides** — RP's COPY-DECK.md, DESIGN-DIRECTIONS.md, DNS-AND-ANALYTICS.md, DEPLOY-NOTES.md and DD's AGENTS.md get no disposition rows (referenced only inside reasons).
6. **DD `auth-attacher.ts`** — sibling of the listed auth-middleware.ts in the SSR scaffolding row; not named (minor, arguably covered).
7. **DD's committed `.env`** — contains the anon/publishable key (not a secret violation, but the decommission row doesn't note the key is in git history, relevant if the project is kept alive at all).
8. **Structural gap:** no "merge" or "rebuild" rows exist in the delivered JSON, and the JSON is truncated after the "Partner apply flow" row — the caller should confirm whether rows were lost in transport.

Verdict: 12/12 sampled rows ACCURATE (4 trivial off-by-one line counts, one ~19-vs-18 CHECK count, one slightly overstated "broken by construction" characterization — none change any disposition). The matrix is grounded; the notable omissions are the two GitHub workflows (especially the keepalive cron) and the DD image assets.
